factors: I guess a lot of what I was trying to figure out was the extent to which the wizard exercised agency, and how that relates to what the farmer was going to do.  If the farmer was going to do something and the wizard didn't get in the way, I don't think the wizard caused or allowed anything.  If the wizard changed something to open a path, and if the farmer pursued that, then the wizard enabled or allowed it.  What's interesting is that I was thinking in terms of the "other" fruit for some of the questions, and my answer might change if the answer choices were re-framed in terms of the other fruit in any given situation.
